title: Text Highlighting
text: Text Highlighting Text Highlighting automatically highlights the instances of selected or caret-based text within the code editor, helping developers quickly spot relevant text and tokens. Caret-based Highlighting: When your caret is positioned on a token, all occurrences of that token are highlighted. This enables you to instantly see where and how that token is used in the code. Selection-based Highlighting: When you select a word or phrase by double-clicking or dragging your cursor, all exact textual matches are highlighted. This is particularly useful for tracking string literals, matching words, or repeated text in the code and comments. There are three properties you can configure for the Text Highlighting feature within the SourceEdit definition inside the [widgets] section in the usys.ini file – TextHighlight , TextHighlightMatchCase , and TextHighlightShade as shown in the following example: Explain this code [widgets] SourceEdit=usrcedit(…;TextHighlight=on;TextHighlightMatchCase=off;TextHighlightShade=black) Copy Table 1. Text Higlighting Properties Description Property Description Default value TextHighlight By setting TextHighlight you can switch on and off the Text Highlighting feature. on TextHighlightMatchCase The TextHighlightMatchCase property controls whether the text matching for highlighting is case-sensitive. off TextHighlightShade The TextHighlightShade property allows customization of the highlight color for the matching occurrences. Black Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
